1_beta_lactam_streptogramin_dominance.explanation: blaA+vat(F) co-occurrence drives these numbers — both are core in Y. enterocolitica

2_dramatic_temporal_surge.interpretation: 10-fold increase from pre-2000 to 2020+. Partly sequencing bias (more clinical isolates sequenced post-2010) but also genuine AMR selection. Requires rarefaction analysis.

3_geographic_hotspots.UK_98.9pct: Highest AMR prevalence — dominated by clinical Y. enterocolitica
3_geographic_hotspots.Russia_10pct: Low AMR — environmental Y. pestis from Siberia (no antibiotic pressure)
3_geographic_hotspots.China_Mongolia_5.6pct: Lowest — Y. pestis plague surveillance (wildlife, no treatment)
3_geographic_hotspots.interpretation: Geographic AMR pattern perfectly tracks clinical vs wildlife surveillance: countries with dominant clinical sequencing show near-100% AMR; countries sequencing wildlife/environmental isolates show near-0%

4_host_AMR_gradient.Pig_93.8pct: blaA/vat(F) core + SA cluster acquired genes
4_host_AMR_gradient.Human_88.7pct: Clinical Y. enterocolitica — blaA/vat(F) + additional acquired
4_host_AMR_gradient.Cattle_57.1pct: Mixed — some Y. enterocolitica, some other species
4_host_AMR_gradient.Rodent_Marmot_1.7pct: Near-zero — Y. pestis from wildlife, no AMR pressure
4_host_AMR_gradient.interpretation: AMR gradient follows antibiotic use intensity: aquaculture (high beta-lactam use) → pig farming (high use) → human clinical (high use) → wildlife (no use)

5_aac6_in_mollaretii.finding: aac(6') in 62.2% of Y. mollaretii genomes
5_aac6_in_mollaretii.significance: aac(6') = aminoglycoside acetyltransferase — modifies gentamicin/tobramycin. Y. mollaretii is rarely studied but carries significant acquired AMR. This is the 'surveillance-neglected species' with cryptic mobilome referenced in Paper 1 title.

6_novel_species_blaFONA.interpretation: blaFONA in the novel circumpolar clade = horizontal gene transfer from co-occurring environmental bacteria in Arctic permafrost/water. Demonstrates environmental AMR acquisition in the cryptic novel species. The one genome carrying blaFONA is from Franz Josef Land (Arctic archipelago) — a geographically isolated environment where blaFONA-carrying Serratia may co-exist.

7_pseudotuberculosis_minimal_amr.interpretation: Y. pseudotuberculosis rarely acquires AMR despite being a human pathogen. Likely reflects its ecology (animal reservoir, sporadic human cases) vs Y. enterocolitica (food chain, consistent human exposure)

paper_figures.Fig1: Genus-wide ANI heatmap + novel species circumpolar distribution
paper_figures.Fig2: Marker gene saturation — all 5 markers vs ANI comparison
paper_figures.Fig3: Species AMR profile matrix (heatmap) — 29 species × 69 genes
